titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Reuben L. and Marcionella V. Curtiss Papers
Curtiss (Reuben L. and Marcionella V.) Papers
titlestmt.author: compiled by Susan Grigg and Françoise Rosset

controlnote.p: This encoded finding aid is compliant with the Yale EAD Best Practice Guidelines, Version 1.0.

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Friday November 13, 2020 at 09:42

did.unittitle: Reuben L. and Marcionella V. Curtiss papers
did.abstract: Correspondence between Reuben L. and Marcionella V. Curtiss during World War II when each was serving in the United States Army make up the bulk of the papers. Reuben L. Curtiss's letters are sent from various parts of the United States, Africa, India, and China. Marcionella V. Curtiss's letters reflect her service in Australia, New Guinea and the Philippine Islands. There is also a diary kept by Reuben L. Curtiss (1944-1945), and a biographical sketch written by Marcionella V. Curtiss in 1979.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Reuben L. and Marcionella V. Curtiss Papers (MS 853). Manuscripts and Archives, Yale University Library.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Reuben L. and Marcionella V. Curtiss Papers consist mainly of correspondence between Mr. and Mrs. Curtiss during their service in the United States Army in the Second World War. Mr. Curtiss wrote his letters mainly from army posts in the United States (September 1942-July 1943) and from India and China (August 1943-June 1945). Mrs. Curtiss wrote her letters from Milford, Connecticut (September-November 1942), from army posts in the United States (December 1942-June 1944), and from Australia, New Guinea, and the Philippines (June 1944-June 1945). The correspondence is most valuable for its descriptions and criticisms of army life and of the people and customs of the places in which the Curtisses were stationed. There is also a diary kept by Mr. Curtiss between February 1944 and December 1945.

The essential biographical source is a 3000-word letter that Mrs. Curtiss (now married to Leland Smith) wrote in February 1979 at the invitation of this department. It is boxed at the beginning of the papers. It not only provides information about parentage, schooling, employment, and the like, but also creates a vivid and moving impression of "Curt & Marcy - Their Life Together." This letter is essential to a full appreciation of the papers. For quick reference, however, please see the chronology selected from it in the Biographical Sketch above.

chronitemset.event: born in North Branford, Connecticut
left school after the sixth grade
held various jobs in New York City and Connecticut
married Marcionella Vegas
bought a campsite in Milford, Connecticut, and began living there except in the winters
built a cabin on the Milford campsite and made it the Curtisses' permanent home
joined the United States Army
served in India and China, supervising transport of American war materiel
left the army
held various jobs in Connecticut
fathered a son, Scott Dewitt
died in the Veterans Administration Medical Center in West Haven, Connecticut
born in Forteleza, Brazil, of American-born parents
came to live in New York
attended junior high school and one month of business school
worked as a "girl friday" for Reuben Curtiss and attended high school in the evenings
married Reuben Curtiss
held various jobs in New York City and Connecticut
joined the United States Army
served in Australia, New Guinea, and the Philippines
left the army
held various jobs in Connecticut
gave birth to a son, Scott Dewitt
held various jobs in Connecticut
separated from Reuben Curtiss and went to live with his nephew, Leland Smith
moved to the Southwest
married Leland Smith

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Copyright status for collection materials is unknown. Transmission or reproduction of materials protected by U.S. Copyright Law (Title 17, U.S.C.) beyond that allowed by fair use requires the written permission of the copyright owners. Works not in the public domain cannot be commercially exploited without permission of the copyright owners. Responsibility for any use rests exclusively with the user.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Reuben L. and Marcionella V. Curtiss Papers were given to Yale University Library by Reuben L. Curtiss in 1975.

did.unittitle: Africa and Middle East (en route to India)
